name: Dodge Roll
description: A committed evasive movement action that briefly changes position, hit reaction rules, and recovery timing, often with invulnerability frames.
design_notes: Dodge roll sits between movement and defense. Define the active invulnerability window, recovery frames, direction capture, collision shape, and cancel rules before tuning displacement. It should reward anticipation and positioning without becoming a universal answer to every attack pattern.
design_purpose: Give players a defensive repositioning tool with readable commitment and recovery.
Create timing skill around attack telegraphs, invulnerability, and spacing.
Connect movement, stamina, combat, and animation locks through one evasive action.
player_fantasy: The player feels nimble under pressure, narrowly avoiding danger while committing to a visible recovery.

parameters.name: roll_displacement_units
parameters.description: Horizontal displacement target before collision truncation.

parameters.name: dodge_roll_i_frames_ms
parameters.description: Milliseconds of eligible damage immunity inside the roll.

parameters.name: dodge_roll_recovery_ms
parameters.description: Post-movement lockout before ordinary actions resume.

parameters.name: roll_input_buffer_ms
parameters.description: Queued input window before roll activation becomes legal.

parameters.name: roll_stamina_drain_units
parameters.description: Stamina resource consumed on accepted roll activation.

parameters.name: roll_capsule_profile_id
parameters.description: Collision profile used during active roll frames.

edge_cases: Roll direction should be captured at activation, not drift when lock-on target or camera heading changes mid-roll.
Incoming damage during the first or last roll frame needs consistent inclusive or exclusive immunity rules.
Rolling into stairs, ledges, or narrow doors must not leave the character inside collision after recovery.
Networked rolls need server authority for immunity and correction for predicted displacement.
common_bugs: Invulnerability is tied to animation events and desyncs when animation playback rate changes.
Roll hits the same hazard twice because damage immunity and hurtbox state are not updated atomically.
Input buffering triggers a roll after stun recovery even though the player released the button long ago.
Capsule profile changes are not restored after interrupt, leaving the character permanently compact.
balancing_notes: Long i-frame windows make roll a timing solution; long displacement makes it a spacing solution.
Recovery frames are the main counterweight when roll is used in combat, especially against repeated attacks.
Stamina cost should reflect how often encounters require rolling rather than only the roll displacement.
accessibility_notes: Use generous input buffering and readable attack telegraphs if roll timing is central to survival.
Consider assist settings that widen dodge_roll_i_frames_ms or reduce repeated-button strain.

implementation_notes.unity: Create a roll state with activation tick, captured direction, immunity interval, and recovery interval. Drive displacement from FixedUpdate with swept collision; damage queries should read dodge_roll_i_frames_ms from state, not from Animator timing alone.
implementation_notes.godot: Store roll_state on the character and update velocity in _physics_process. Use animation callbacks only to request visual transitions; collision profile and invulnerability should be changed by deterministic state ticks.
implementation_notes.unreal: Implement roll as a Gameplay Ability or custom CharacterMovement state. Server validates stamina and immunity windows, while predicted clients play the montage and reconcile displacement when authoritative movement arrives.
implementation_notes.web: Represent roll as an immutable action instance with activation_tick, direction, displacement, and recovery. Resolve per-target damage immunity before collision and animation rendering.

relationships.reason: Dodge roll often spends stamina to limit repeated evasive actions and create recovery pressure.
Many dodge-roll designs depend on a timed immunity window resolved by the combat damage system.
Recovery and hit-stun priority determine whether dodge roll can escape pressure or is punished after mistiming.

example_games.title: Dark Souls
example_games.note: Uses dodge rolling as a core defensive timing and positioning action.

example_games.title: Enter the Gungeon
example_games.note: Uses dodge roll for projectile evasion with committed recovery.
